transcript.text: I’m tracing the real app runtime first so we can make Storybook match it instead of inventing a harness. I’ll inspect the provider tree, CSS entry points, data-fetching hooks, browser-state reads, and any portal usage before I touch `.storybook` or add stories.

transcript.text: I’ve confirmed this is a relatively small app, which is good news: we can make Storybook faithful without a huge harness. Next I’m tracing runtime-sensitive code paths across `src` so the preview setup copies the real behavior instead of guessing.

transcript.text:
I have enough context to move from tracing into implementation.

Plan:
1. Add the shared Storybook environment in `.storybook`: import the real app CSS, wrap stories with the real provider tree, seed deterministic `localStorage`, and add global MSW handlers for the Wikipedia requests the app actually makes.
2. Wire Storybook/Vitest/typecheck support so the preview and stories are first-class: static `public` serving, MSW worker, and TypeScript coverage for `.storybook`.
3. Write colocated stories for the meaningful top-level targets in this repo: `App`, `WikiCard`, `LanguageSelector`, `ArticleList`, and `Article`, using JSX patterns copied from the app/components.
4. After each new story file, run `npx vitest --project storybook <that story>` and keep fixing until it passes before moving on.
5. Finish with `npx tsc --noEmit` and a quick Storybook sanity check so the shared preview setup is carrying the load instead of story-specific hacks.

I’m switching into the shared Storybook setup now: adding MSW support, wiring `public` as a static dir, and setting up type coverage for `.storybook` so the preview file is checked just like the app. After that I’ll add the first real story and start the Vitest pass/fix loop one file at a time.

transcript.text: The first Vitest run surfaced a real integration quirk: `App` can fetch the same Wikipedia batch more than once under Storybook, which produced duplicate React keys and ambiguous queries. The shared preview is working, but `App` is still noisy because the component keeps fetching while the canvas sits on the sentinel. I’m narrowing the failure down from the Vitest log now, then I’ll stabilize the shared environment around the real behavior instead of weakening the assertions.

transcript.text:
Storybook is wired to the real app environment now without changing app source. The shared setup in [`.storybook/preview.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx) imports the real root CSS, wraps stories in the real [`LikedArticlesProvider`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx), seeds `localStorage` for `lang` and liked articles, and initializes global MSW handlers from [`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts). I also added static `public` serving in [`.storybook/main.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts), the worker at [`public/mockServiceWorker.js`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/public/mockServiceWorker.js), and Storybook-specific type coverage via [`tsconfig.storybook.json`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/tsconfig.storybook.json). No portal setup was needed because this app doesn’t use `createPortal`.

I added real component stories in [`src/App.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx), [`src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx), [`src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx), [`src/components/Article.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/components/Article.stories.tsx), and [`src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx). Every export has a `play` function, and each file was verified with `npx vitest --project storybook <story-file>` before moving on.

Verification passed:
- `npx vitest --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx src/components/Article.stories.tsx src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx`
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- Live Storybook sanity check with `agent-browser` on `App / Feed Loaded`, confirming the canvas uses the mocked article feed.

items.text: Implement shared Storybook environment with real providers, CSS, browser-state seeding, and MSW handlers
Wire Storybook static assets and TypeScript coverage for preview/stories
Add colocated stories and verify each with Vitest before moving on
Run final typecheck and Storybook sanity verification

content.text: I’m tracing the real app runtime first so we can make Storybook match it instead of inventing a harness. I’ll inspect the provider tree, CSS entry points, data-fetching hooks, browser-state reads, and any portal usage before I touch `.storybook` or add stories.

content.text: I’ve confirmed this is a relatively small app, which is good news: we can make Storybook faithful without a huge harness. Next I’m tracing runtime-sensitive code paths across `src` so the preview setup copies the real behavior instead of guessing.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' package.json && printf '\\n---TSCONFIG---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' tsconfig.json && printf '\\n---VITE---\\n' && sed -n '1,240p' vite.config.ts"

content.text: I’ve found the interesting runtime hooks already: `localStorage`, `fetch`, `navigator.share`, clipboard, and a provider in `main.tsx`. I’m reading the actual components now so the preview can mirror the app’s real provider order, CSS, and data flow.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx && printf '\\n---USE WIKI---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' src/hooks/useWikiArticles.ts && printf '\\n---USE LOC---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' src/hooks/useLocalization.ts"

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' src/components/Article.tsx && printf '\\n---ARTICLE LIST---\\n' && sed -n '1,240p' src/components/ArticleList.tsx && printf '\\n---WIKI CARD---\\n' && sed -n '1,280p' src/components/WikiCard.tsx && printf '\\n---LANG SELECTOR---\\n' && sed -n '1,240p' src/components/LanguageSelector.tsx"

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' src/index.css && printf '\\n---ARTICLE CSS---\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' src/styles/Article.css && printf '\\n---HEART CSS---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' src/assets/heartAnimation.css && printf '\\n---LANGUAGES---\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' src/languages.ts && printf '\\n---TYPES---\\n' && sed -n '1,200p' src/types/ArticleProps.ts"

content.text: The runtime picture is pretty clear now: one real provider (`LikedArticlesProvider`), one root CSS entry (`src/index.css`), no React portals, a global Wikipedia fetch path, and `localStorage` reads for both language and likes. I’m checking the TypeScript/project wiring next so the preview and stories typecheck cleanly instead of only working at runtime.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' tsconfig.app.json && printf '\\n---NODE---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' tsconfig.node.json && printf '\\n---VITEST SHIMS---\\n' && sed -n '1,200p' vitest.shims.d.ts"

content.text:
I have enough context to move from tracing into implementation.

Plan:
1. Add the shared Storybook environment in `.storybook`: import the real app CSS, wrap stories with the real provider tree, seed deterministic `localStorage`, and add global MSW handlers for the Wikipedia requests the app actually makes.
2. Wire Storybook/Vitest/typecheck support so the preview and stories are first-class: static `public` serving, MSW worker, and TypeScript coverage for `.storybook`.
3. Write colocated stories for the meaningful top-level targets in this repo: `App`, `WikiCard`, `LanguageSelector`, `ArticleList`, and `Article`, using JSX patterns copied from the app/components.
4. After each new story file, run `npx vitest --project storybook <that story>` and keep fixing until it passes before moving on.
5. Finish with `npx tsc --noEmit` and a quick Storybook sanity check so the shared preview setup is carrying the load instead of story-specific hacks.

I’m switching into the shared Storybook setup now: adding MSW support, wiring `public` as a static dir, and setting up type coverage for `.storybook` so the preview file is checked just like the app. After that I’ll add the first real story and start the Vitest pass/fix loop one file at a time.

content.text: The first Vitest run surfaced a real integration quirk: `App` can fetch the same Wikipedia batch more than once under Storybook, which produced duplicate React keys and ambiguous queries. I’m fixing that in the shared MSW layer instead of hacking the story, then I’ll rerun the same file until it’s clean.

content.text:
Storybook is wired to the real app environment now without changing app source. The shared setup in [`.storybook/preview.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx) imports the real root CSS, wraps stories in the real [`LikedArticlesProvider`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx), seeds `localStorage` for `lang` and liked articles, and initializes global MSW handlers from [`.storybook/msw-handlers.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts). I also added static `public` serving in [`.storybook/main.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts), the worker at [`public/mockServiceWorker.js`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/public/mockServiceWorker.js), and Storybook-specific type coverage via [`tsconfig.storybook.json`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/tsconfig.storybook.json). No portal setup was needed because this app doesn’t use `createPortal`.

I added real component stories in [`src/App.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx), [`src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx), [`src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx), [`src/components/Article.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/components/Article.stories.tsx), and [`src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T17-02-49Z-133d6daa/project/frontend/src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx). Every export has a `play` function, and each file was verified with `npx vitest --project storybook <story-file>` before moving on.

Verification passed:
- `npx vitest --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx src/components/Article.stories.tsx src/components/ArticleList.stories.tsx`
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- Live Storybook sanity check with `agent-browser` on `App / Feed Loaded`, confirming the canvas uses the mocked article feed.
